item.title: America’s Pacific Allies Train to Face Down China Together
item.description: Seven nations have deployed a total of 17,000 personnel for an annual exercise in the Philippines that has angered Beijing.

item.title: China’s ‘Teapot’ Refiners, Targeted by U.S., Offer Financial Lifeline to Iran
item.description: A sector that once rebelled against Beijing has grown into a critical tool for working around sanctions.

item.title: Taiwan Outfoxes China in Test of Wills Over Tiny African Country
item.description: The island’s leader scored a point in the battle for recognition, surfacing in Eswatini despite Beijing’s objections.

item.title: China’s Factory Activity Holds Up Despite Middle East Disruptions
item.description: China’s factory activity held up in April, suggesting limited pressure from surging energy prices due to the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Rare-Earth Stocks Rise as China Looks to Tighten Production Oversight
item.description: Beijing is seeking to tighten controls on production of the minerals used in everything from cars to defense equipment.

item.title: China’s Ban on Meta-Manus Deal Shows Strains in U.S.-China Business Ties
item.description: China’s move to force Meta to unwind its acquisition of the AI startup demonstrates Beijing’s willingness to use economic weapons to safeguard key interests.

item.title: China Says Hostile Foreign Forces Are Driving Its Youth to Slack Off
item.description: The country’s intelligence agency blamed anti-China influences from abroad for stoking youth disillusionment.

item.title: When America and China Were Friendlier—and 10,000 Soldiers Built a Bike Track
item.description: Plus, a U.S. president’s unfavorable first impression of a lower-ranking Xi Jinping.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.

item.title: The U.S. Wants to Break China’s Drone Dominance. Here’s Where It Will Struggle.
item.description: A part-by-part breakdown of a typical first-person-view drone shows the strength of China’s stranglehold.
